introSection: Indy's Lost World was a steel kiddie roller coaster built by UK manufacturer Supercar previously located at Southport Pleasureland in Southport, Merseyside, England, UK.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride originated at a unknown location before opening at South Shore Holiday Village in the 1990s. It closed in 2005 when the park closed its amusement ride section.

The coaster was moved to New Pleasureland Southport in 2008 and became an indoor roller coaster situated in the park's former Fun House building, entitled Indy's Lost World, although various sources have named the coaster Indiana's Lost World or Indiana's Adventure - The Ride.

The ride was removed from the park in 2010, with a Ghost Train eventually moving into the building.

fullText:
Indy's Lost World was a steel kiddie roller coaster built by UK manufacturer Supercar previously located at Southport Pleasureland in Southport, Merseyside, England, UK.

The ride originated at a unknown location before opening at South Shore Holiday Village in the 1990s. It closed in 2005 when the park closed its amusement ride section.

The coaster was moved to New Pleasureland Southport in 2008 and became an indoor roller coaster situated in the park's former Fun House building, entitled Indy's Lost World, although various sources have named the coaster Indiana's Lost World or Indiana's Adventure - The Ride.

The ride was removed from the park in 2010, with a Ghost Train eventually moving into the building.
